drugs.efficacy_snippet: 14.3 Active Lupus Nephritis Study Design and Population The efficacy of GAZYVA was evaluated in REGENCY (NCT04221477), a Phase III, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-group, multicenter study in 271 patients with ISN/RPS 2003 Class III or IV, with or without concomitant Class V l

key_results: Patients were randomized 1:1 to receive GAZYVA 1,000 mg (N=135) or placebo (N=136) intravenously, in combination with MMF 2-2.5 g/day and a tapering course of corticosteroids and were evaluated over 76 weeks.

Patients randomized to receive GAZYVA were further randomized in a 1:1 ratio to receive either GAZYVA 1,000 mg IV on Day 1, Weeks 2, 24, 26, 50, and 52 (Arm 1), or GAZYVA 1,000 mg IV on Day 1, Weeks 2, 24, 26, and 52 (Arm 2).
The distribution by kidney biopsy class was 39% Class III, 61% Class IV and 31% had concomitant Class V.
efficacy_card.source_section: 14.3 Active Lupus Nephritis Study Design and Population The efficacy of GAZYVA was evaluated in REGENCY (NCT04221477), a Pha

safety_card.boxed_warning: WARNING: HEPATITIS B VIRUS REACTIVATION and PROGRESSIVE MULTIFOCAL LEUKOENCEPHALOPATHY Hepatitis B Virus (HBV) reactivation, in some cases resulting in fulminant hepatitis, hepatic failure, and death, can occur in patients receiving CD20-directed cytolytic antibodies, including GAZYVA. Screen all patients for HBV infection before treatment initiation. Monitor HBV-positive patients during and after

drugs.efficacy_snippet: 14.1 Intravenous Administration in Adults with Moderate to Severe SLE Trial Design and Population The safety and efficacy of SAPHNELO were evaluated in three 52-week treatment period, multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trials (Trial 1 [NCT01438489], Trial 2 [NCT02446912] and T

key_results: The primary endpoint was a combined assessment of the SRI-4 and the sustained reduction in OCS (<10 mg/day and ≤OCS dose at Week 1, sustained for 12 weeks) measured at Week 24.
The primary endpoints were improvement in disease activity evaluated at 52 weeks, measured by SRI-4 in Trial 2 and BICLA in Trial 3 (defined above).

drugs.efficacy_snippet: 14 CLINICAL STUDIES The effectiveness of Acthar Gel as a treatment for infantile spasms was demonstrated in a single blinded (video EEG interpreter blinded) clinical trial in which patients were randomized to receive either a 2-week course of treatment with Acthar Gel (75 U/m 2 intramuscular twice d

key_results: 14 CLINICAL STUDIES The effectiveness of Acthar Gel as a treatment for infantile spasms was demonstrated in a single blinded (video EEG interpreter blinded) clinical trial in which patients were randomized to receive either a 2-week course of treatment with Acthar Gel (75 U/m 2 intramuscular twice daily) or prednisone (1 mg/kg by mouth twice daily).
Thirteen of 15 patients (86.7%) responded to Acthar Gel as compared to 4 of 14 patients (28.6%) given prednisone (p<0.002).
Seven of 8 patients (87.5%) responded to Acthar Gel after not responding to prednisone.

safety_card.common_adverse_reactions: 6 ADVERSE REACTIONS Common adverse reactions for corticosteroids include fluid retention, alteration in glucose tolerance, elevation in blood pressure, behavioral and mood changes, increased appetite

drugs.efficacy_snippet: 14 CLINICAL STUDIES The safety and efficacy of LUPKYNIS were investigated in Study 1 (NCT03021499), a 52-week, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial in patients with a diagnosis of systemic lupus erythematosus and with International Society of Nephrology / Renal Pathology Society (ISN/R

key_results: 14 CLINICAL STUDIES The safety and efficacy of LUPKYNIS were investigated in Study 1 (NCT03021499), a 52-week, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial in patients with a diagnosis of systemic lupus erythematosus and with International Society of Nephrology / Renal Pathology Society (ISN/RPS) biopsy-proven active Class III or IV LN (alone or in combination with Class V LN) or Class V LN.
A total of 357 patients with LN were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to receive either LUPKYNIS 23.7 mg twice daily or placebo.

safety_card.boxed_warning: WARNING: MALIGNANCIES AND SERIOUS INFECTIONS Increased risk for developing malignancies and serious infections with LUPKYNIS or other immunosuppressants that may lead to hospitalization or death [see Warnings and Precautions ( 5.1 , 5.2 )].
